home.intro1: Where longevity science meets advanced AI.

home.intro4: From AI-guided advice to expert consultation, from cutting-edge research to verified products—your complete longevity ecosystem, driven by science, technology, evidence, and expertise.

offlinePayment.title: Offline payment (Alipay / WeChat)
offlinePayment.intro: If you cannot pay with Visa/Mastercard via Stripe, follow this offline transfer and manual activation flow.
offlinePayment.section1Body: Contact support (e.g. from Account) for the latest Alipay/WeChat QR code or account. Codes may be updated periodically.
offlinePayment.section2Body: Transfer the agreed amount for your chosen membership or service (Standard, Premium, courses, consultation, TCM prevention, etc.) via Alipay or WeChat.
offlinePayment.section2Item1: Full payment screenshot (time, amount, transaction ID)
offlinePayment.section2Item2: The email you used to register
offlinePayment.section3Body: Send the above to platform admin (choose one):
offlinePayment.section3Item1: Email to support with screenshot, registered email, and service type.
offlinePayment.section3Item2: WeChat or other official channel with screenshot and details.
offlinePayment.section3Note: After verification, your account will be upgraded within the agreed time (e.g. 24 hours).
offlinePayment.section4Item1: Check your membership and access in Account.
offlinePayment.section4Item2: Visit the relevant modules to confirm access.
offlinePayment.section5Body: Offline payment is a supplement to Stripe. Only use official payment details. Verify any suspicious contact through official channels.
offlinePayment.backOnline: You can also return to the
offlinePayment.backOnlineSuffix: to pay with Visa/Mastercard via Stripe.

longevityNews.title: Longevity Medical News
longevityNews.subtitle: Monthly high-impact longevity research from leading journals.

1.title: Senolytic drugs reduce biomarkers of aging in humans
1.summary: First human trial showing clearance of senescent cells improves physical function and inflammatory markers.

2.title: Gut microbiome and longevity: meta-analysis of centenarian cohorts
2.summary: Consistent signatures of gut microbiota associated with extreme longevity across multiple populations.

3.title: Intermittent fasting and cardiovascular outcomes: 5-year follow-up
3.summary: Time-restricted eating linked to lower incidence of major cardiovascular events in observational cohort.

4.title: NAD+ precursors and muscle function in older adults
4.summary: Randomized trial of NR supplementation on mitochondrial function and physical performance.

5.title: Epigenetic clocks and all-cause mortality prediction
5.summary: Comparison of second-generation clocks in large prospective cohorts.

6.title: Rapamycin and immune function in healthy elderly
6.summary: Low-dose rapamycin improves vaccine response and reduces infection in older adults.

tcmPrevention.title: TCM · Preventive Care
tcmPrevention.subtitle: Chinese herbal medicine and classic preventive formulas: property, efficacy, suitable groups, and sources.

1.name: Astragalus
1.property: Sweet, mildly warm. Spleen, Lung meridians.
1.efficacy: Tonify qi, raise yang, secure exterior, stop sweating, promote urination, expel toxin.
1.suitableFor: Qi deficiency, spontaneous sweating, spleen deficiency, sinking qi.
1.caution: Use with caution in excess patterns, stagnation, damp obstruction, yin deficiency with yang hyperactivity.

2.name: Codonopsis
2.efficacy: Tonify spleen and lung, nourish blood and fluids.
2.suitableFor: Spleen-lung qi deficiency, fatigue, cough, thirst.
2.caution: Not for excess/heat patterns alone in large dose; incompatible with Veratrum.

3.name: Goji Berry
3.efficacy: Tonify liver and kidney, brighten eyes.
3.suitableFor: Liver-kidney yin deficiency, dizziness, blurred vision, thirst.
3.caution: Use with caution in spleen deficiency, loose stool; not for external excess heat.

4.name: Chinese Yam
4.property: Sweet, neutral. Spleen, Lung, Kidney meridians.
4.efficacy: Tonify spleen, nourish lung, astringe kidney essence.
4.suitableFor: Spleen deficiency, chronic diarrhea, cough, seminal loss, leukorrhea.
4.caution: Use with caution when damp is abundant or excess patterns.

5.name: Poria
5.property: Sweet, bland, neutral. Heart, Spleen, Kidney meridians.
5.efficacy: Drain dampness, strengthen spleen, calm heart.
5.suitableFor: Edema, phlegm-damp dizziness, spleen deficiency, restlessness.
5.caution: Use with caution in yin deficiency without damp-heat, cold deficiency.

6.name: Chrysanthemum
6.property: Pungent, sweet, bitter, slightly cold. Lung, Liver meridians.
6.efficacy: Release wind-heat, calm liver, brighten eyes, clear heat.
6.suitableFor: Wind-heat cold, headache, red eyes, blurred vision.
6.caution: Use with caution in qi deficiency, cold stomach, loose stool.

1.name: Jade Screen Powder
1.efficacy: Boost qi, secure exterior, stop sweating.
1.suitableFor: Exterior deficiency, spontaneous sweating, susceptibility to colds.

2.name: Six-Ingredient Rehmannia Pill
2.suitableFor: Kidney yin deficiency, dizziness, tinnitus, low back pain, night sweats.

3.name: Four Gentlemen Decoction
3.suitableFor: Spleen-stomach qi deficiency, fatigue, poor appetite, loose stool.

4.name: Center-Supplementing Qi-Boosting Decoction
4.suitableFor: Spleen qi sinking, prolapse, chronic diarrhea.

5.name: Pulse-Engendering Powder
5.efficacy: Tonify qi, generate fluids, astringe sweat.
5.suitableFor: Qi-yin damage, profuse sweating, fatigue, dry throat.

6.name: Free Wanderer Powder
6.efficacy: Soothe liver, nourish blood, strengthen spleen.
6.suitableFor: Liver qi stagnation with blood deficiency, rib pain, dizziness, irregular menses.
